subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 05
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering science, geography, environment, and daily life for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: What is the hardest mineral found on Earth?
questions.explanation: Diamond is the hardest known natural material on the Mohs scale.
questions.hint: It is often used in jewelry.
questions.objective: Learn basic facts about earth and science.

questions.q: Which ocean is located between Africa and Australia?
questions.explanation: The Indian Ocean is the third-largest of the world's oceanic divisions.
questions.hint: It is named after the country of India.

questions.q: What is the process called when water turns into steam or vapor?
questions.explanation: Evaporation is when liquid water changes into gas form due to heat.

questions.q: Which food group provides calcium for strong bones?
questions.explanation: Dairy products like milk, cheese, and yogurt are excellent sources of calcium.
questions.hint: Think of things that come from cows.

questions.q: What is the name of the imaginary line that divides the Earth into Northern and Southern Hemispheres?
questions.explanation: The equator is the line at 0 degrees latitude that circles the Earth.

questions.q: Which of these animals is nocturnal (active at night)?
questions.explanation: Nocturnal animals are active during the night and sleep during the day.
questions.hint: It is a bird known for saying 'hoo'.

questions.q: What is the main purpose of a traffic light?
options: To control traffic flow and safety
questions.answer: To control traffic flow and safety
questions.explanation: Traffic lights coordinate the movement of cars to prevent accidents.
questions.hint: It has red, yellow, and green colors.

questions.q: Which gas do plants absorb from the air?
questions.explanation: Plants take in carbon dioxide to perform photosynthesis and release oxygen.

questions.q: Which of these is a form of renewable energy?
questions.explanation: Solar energy comes from the sun and is constantly renewed.

questions.q: Which sport uses a racket and a shuttlecock?
questions.explanation: Badminton is a game played with rackets and a shuttlecock over a net.
questions.hint: It is often played by two or four people.
